text: Code reviews are an essential practice in software development. A code review involves one or more developers examining another developer's code before it is added to the main project. This process helps catch bugs early, when they are much easier and cheaper to fix. Beyond finding mistakes, code reviews are a great tool for knowledge sharing. Team members learn from each other's coding styles, techniques, and solutions to problems. This shared understanding leads to a more consistent codebase and reduces the risk when a developer is unavailable. Ultimately, regular code reviews improve overall team quality, build trust, and help everyone become a better programmer.
